stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • A supply chain is the linked sequence of producers, transporters, and sellers that brings a product to market.
  • Geographer Lisa Carbone studies the modern supply chain of vanilla.
  • At one end of the vanilla chain are smallholder farmers in Madagascar.
  • At the other end are bakeries and dessert manufacturers in France that purchase the high-priced cured vanilla.
  • According to Carbone, this chain emerged in the 1990s as European demand surged.
stem: The student wants to provide an overview of the vanilla supply chain. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Decades after European demand surged, a supply chain now connects smallholder vanilla farmers in Madagascar with bakeries and dessert manufacturers in France that buy the cured beans at high prices.
Supply chains include the linked production and sale of products, such as vanilla, on the international market.
The modern vanilla supply chain originated in the 1990s when, according to Carbone, European demand for the spice surged.
Bakeries and dessert manufacturers in France that purchase high-priced cured vanilla sit at one end of the supply chain.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. It provides a complete overview by naming both ends of the supply chain (Madagascar farmers and French manufacturers) and situating the chain in time.

Choice B is incorrect because it gives only a generic definition. Choice C is incorrect because it covers only the chain's origin, not the full chain. Choice D is incorrect because it describes only one end of the chain.
